data.title: Current US president to be first to have his signature featured on US currency.
data.description: The current president is set to become the first in-office president to have his signature on the American currency.

data.title: Regulatory Expert Files Citizen Petition Urging FDA to Address 13-Year Delay in Conflict-of-Interest Policy
data.description: A regulatory science expert and adjunct professor at the University of Illinois Chicago, Sarfaraz K. Niazi, Ph.D., reported today the submission of a formal Citizen Petition to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration under 21 CFR §10.30. The petition calls for certain actions and adjustments in the FDA regulations.

data.title: Faraday Future Completes Delivery of Additional EAI Robots to New PBB Auto Inc., and Showcases Its Robotics to Students in Los Angeles Through an Innovative Hands-on Interactive Demonstration Event
data.description: In Los Angeles, Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc. (Nasdaq: FFAI) revealed the delivery of its Master robot and the pilot delivery of the Aegis robot to New PBB Auto Inc. The California-based EAI company sealed a binding FX B2B deposit agreement with New PBB Auto last year. data.title: Texas Fluid Power Expands Nationwide Hydraulic Repair and Rebuild Services for Industrial and Mobile Equipment
data.description: Houston's Texas Fluid Power, a leading provider of hydraulic rebuilds, fluids, and fluid filtration services, is set to extend its reach across the United States. The company, headquartered in Houston, has announced plans to broaden its service capabilities to cater to businesses operating in crucial industrial sectors nationwide. This expansion aims to enhance support for companies in various regions with a focus on delivering maximum machine reliability. Additional details about the scope and timeline of this nationwide expansion were not provided.

data.title: Go Behind the Science During Fusion Energy Week: Tour the DIII-D National Fusion Facility, the Nation’s Largest Operating Fusion Device, and See the Future of Energy in Action
data.description: General Atomics and the DIII-D National Fusion Facility are collaborating to harness the power of fusion, the same process that fuels the sun and stars, for sustainable energy on Earth. At the DIII-D facility, operated by General Atomics for the U.S. Department of Energy Office of Science, researchers are dedicated to making fusion a viable energy source. As part of Fusion Energy Week in May, the facility will offer a unique opportunity for the public to explore the cutting-edge research being conducted there. This initiative aims to educate and engage guests about the potential of fusion energy.
